label: Days to Kickoff
deltaNote: Opening match: Mexico vs South Africa at Estadio Azteca

label: FIFA Projected Revenue
contestNote: FIFA 2023–2026 budget projects $11B total; independent analysts cite ~$10.9B for tournament-specific revenue; economists warn travel bans and immigration enforcement may reduce actual returns below projections

label: Expected International Visitors
contestNote: Economists including Andrew Zimbalist warn US travel bans on 39 countries and aggressive immigration enforcement will suppress visitor numbers and economic returns below FIFA projections

label: Group Stage Groups
deltaNote: 4 teams per group; top 2 + 8 best 3rd-place advance

label: Ticket Applications Received
deltaNote: Initial application window Dec 11, 2025 – Jan 13, 2026; final prices draw widespread fan backlash

label: Final Ticket Top Price (Resale)
source: FIFA Official Resale Platform / Al Jazeera
contestNote: Face value top tier is $8,680; resale on FIFA's own platform reached $143,750 per seat — 41x face value. Fan groups and UEFA critic Henry Winter called tournament at risk of becoming 'Corporate Games'.

label: Countries Under US Travel Ban
source: American Immigration Council / National Immigration Forum
deltaNote: Includes 4 qualified teams: Haiti, Iran, Senegal, Ivory Coast. B-2 tourist visas blocked for fans from all 39 nations.

label: Intercontinental Play-offs
deltaNote: Iraq & DR Congo qualify — last 2 spots filled. Iraq beat Bolivia 2-1 → Group I (France, Senegal, Norway); DR Congo beat Jamaica 1-0 AET → Group K (Portugal, Colombia, Uzbekistan)

label: USMNT Last Result
source: US Soccer / ESPN — Mercedes-Benz Stadium, Atlanta (Mar 31)
deltaNote: Trincão (37'), João Félix (59') for Portugal. USA more shots in 1st half but defensive errors costly. Combined 2-7 across Belgium/Portugal camp. Next: May 31 vs Senegal.
